case_name: People of Michigan v. Brooklynne Rae Davis

summary.brief: People of Michigan v. Brooklynne Rae Davis. Medical malpractice case involving paediatrics.

evidence_index.text: This case arises from allegations that defendant failed to care for her 19-month-old twins, SS and AS, thereby causing the death of SS and threatening the life of AS.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Dr. Cory Schmidt, a medical doctor who was qualified as an expert in pediatrics, testified.
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. Cory Schmidt

evidence_index.text: On August 22, 2024, police officers and medical personnel responded to defendant’s home after defendant called the police and reported a deceased child.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: AS was so malnourished and dehydrated that he had encephalopathy, which meant AS exhibited 'an altered level of consciousness where he was not as awake,' and it was 'hard to keep him awake.'
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. Cory Schmidt

evidence_index.text: Dr. Schmidt further explained that the 'dangerously high' sodium level in AS’s blood was evidence of AS’s dehydration, as was the child’s kidney injury, weight loss, and encephalopathy.
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. Cory Schmidt

evidence_index.text: The district court concluded that first-degree child abuse did not require an affirmative act and that there was probable cause to bind over defendant to the circuit court on that charge.
evidence_index.speaker: District court

evidence_index.text: The purpose of a preliminary examination is to determine whether a crime has been committed, and, if so, whether probable cause exists to believe that the defendant committed it.
evidence_index.speaker: Court analysis

evidence_index.text: Dr. Schmidt testified that if left untreated, AS’s 'fluid balance' was so off that he would have suffered permanent brain damage and would not have developed normally.
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. Cory Schmidt

evidence_index.text: AS weighed approximately 16 or 17 pounds, which was 'very, very low' for his age (19 months) and height.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The district court could appropriately draw inferences from the evidence presented in this case to determine that the prosecution presented sufficient evidence that defendant intentionally or knowingly caused serious physical harm to her child.
evidence_index.speaker: Court conclusion

initial_state.chief_complaint: Altered level of consciousness and severe malnutrition
initial_state.history_of_present_illness: AS was found to be severely malnourished and dehydrated, with a dangerously high sodium level and encephalopathy.

focused_exam.general: Altered level of consciousness, difficult to keep awake
focused_exam.skin: Uncontrolled eczema with excoriated areas on legs

requestables.name: Serum Sodium Level
requestables.description: Measurement of sodium concentration in blood

reveal.decision_impact: Immediate intervention needed to prevent permanent damage

timeline_phases.name: Initial Presentation
timeline_phases.description: AS presented with severe symptoms requiring urgent care

decision_points.clinical_context: AS presented with severe malnutrition and dehydration
decision_points.prompt: What immediate management should be initiated?

options.description: Initiate IV fluid resuscitation and nutritional support
options.clinical_reasoning: Standard care for severe dehydration and malnutrition

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failure to provide timely medical care led to life-threatening condition
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Immediate intervention to prevent further harm

patient_outcome.description: AS suffered severe malnutrition and dehydration, requiring ICU care
patient_outcome.long_term_effects: Potential for permanent brain damage if untreated

key_findings: Defendant failed to provide necessary care
Causation established - lack of care led to life-threatening condition

malpractice_determination.point_of_failure: Failure to provide timely medical care
malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Timely intervention could have prevented severe condition

factual_timeline.event: Police and medical personnel responded to defendant's home

treatments.treatment: ICU care for dehydration and malnutrition
